document.masterDocTitle: Revision of the genus Georissus (Coleoptera, Hydrophiloidea, Georissidae) of Japan

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Revision of the genus Georissus (Coleoptera, Hydrophiloidea, Georissidae) of Japan

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Entomological Laboratory, Faculty of Agriculture, Ehime University, Tarumi 3 - 5 - 7, Matsuyama, 790 - 8566 Japan.
Entomological Laboratory, Faculty of Agriculture, Ehime University, Tarumi 3 - 5 - 7, Matsuyama, 790 - 8566 Japan.

bibRefCitation.author: Sato M.
bibRefCitation.refString: Sato M. 1972. The Georissid beetles of Japan. Journal of Nagoya Women's College 18: 207 - 213.

subSubSection.paragraph: Figs 2C , 3B, H , 4B, E, H–I, N , 5C , 6C , 7C , 8C , 9C ; Table 1

caption.paragraph:
Table 1. Measurements of
Georissus
( G .) spp. and
G . ( Nipponogeorissus) granulosus Satô, 1972
.

subSubSection.paragraph:
This species is easily distinguished from other Japanese species of
Georissus
by the granulated pronotum and shape of male aedeagus; shape elongated and narrow, phallobase widening. This species is distinguished from
Georissus biroi
described from Papua New Guinea by the carinae of frons;
G . granulosus
has transverse carinae, in contrast to their absence in
G . biroi
.

paragraph.materialsCitation:
Holotype
JAPAN –
Honshu : Mie Pref.
• ♀ ; Tsu-shi , Furukawa ;
23 Aug. 1958
; H. Ichihashi leg.; EUMJ ( Fig. 3B, L ).

subSubSection.paragraph:
Other material JAPAN –
Honshu : Mie Pref.
• 5 ex. ; Ise-shi , Iso-cho , Miya-gawa ;
16 Jun. 2008
; K. Akita leg.; EUMJ
.
ELYTRA. Entirely granulate. Elytral intervals 2, 4, 6 and 8 elevated, forming longitudinal carinae. Humeral bulge distinct, bearing granules. Lateral ridges indistinct. Hind wings developed.

MALE GENITALIA ( Fig. 9C ). Aedeagus 0.37 mm long. Parameres slightly shorter than phallobase, basal portions of parameres combined as wide as anterior portion of phallobase; lateral margins. Median lobe shorter than parameres, subparallel-sided, slightly angular at apex; basal struts short. Phallobase about 2.4 times as long as wide, widening posteriorly; posterior portion with large basal foramen.
